goal: Build the first BP-C1 Council Kernel slice as local deterministic Node runtime tools: a council agent client and a Maya council watcher that follow Council Session Protocol v1.1 without starting BP-C2 modules.
scope_out: No auth, secrets, DB, deploy, live Builder, AICOS write, or Soulmatch copy
No creation of real .bluepilot runtime state in the repo
claims: tools/council-agent-client.cjs can register an agent, acknowledge directives, read next directives, and report started, done, or hard_stop status using atomic JSON writes.
tools/maya-council-watcher.cjs can process agent state, deduplicate status events, mark tasks done, pause on hard_stop, assign dependency-ready queued tasks to idle agents, and append JSONL events.
BP-C1 runtime state path remains configurable and tests use temporary directories, not committed .bluepilot state.
No BP-C2 module starts or is implemented.
assumptions: Bluepilot runtime tools should use the repo's current Node CJS pattern rather than introducing TypeScript setup.
The first BP-C1 slice should be deterministic and locally testable before long-running watch mode is added.
Council Session Protocol v1.1 is the source contract for file ownership, directive_cursor, dedup, and HARD STOP behavior.
dod: git diff --name-only contains only allowed files.
stop_conditions: Auth, secrets, DB, deploy, live Builder, AICOS write, or Soulmatch copy becomes necessary
Runtime requires package or lockfile changes
prior_task_findings: BP-086 made Council Session Protocol v1.1 the ready spec for BP-C1. BP-087 added the standing rule to critically review external AI output before implementation.
